task_completion.description: Navigate to the Wikipedia page for Tor (network).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and the address bar displays 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tor_(network)'.

task_completion.description: Capture the native HTML element screenshot of the Software Infobox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser Developer Tools are open with the infobox DOM node selected and the 'Capture node screenshot' option used, or a saved image file named 'tor_wiki_infobox.png' is visible showing exactly the infobox.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Tor Project check page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and the address bar displays 'https://check.torproject.org/'.

task_completion.description: Capture the native HTML element screenshot of the main central status block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser Developer Tools are open with the status block DOM node selected and captured, or a saved image file named 'tor_status_block.png' is visible showing exactly the status block (onion graphic and text).

task_completion.description: Record the two visited URLs in a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'capture_log.txt' that contains both 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tor_(network)' and 'https://check.torproject.org/'.

task_completion.description: Save all files to the specified Forensic_Evidence directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the directory '/home/ga/Documents/Forensic_Evidence' containing 'tor_wiki_infobox.png', 'tor_status_block.png', and 'capture_log.txt'.

integrity.description: Ensure screenshots are native DOM node captures, not full-screen or manual crops.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the browser's built-in 'Capture node screenshot' feature in Developer Tools. The resulting images should have clean boundaries matching the HTML element exactly, with no browser chrome, desktop UI, or uneven manual cropping edges.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually visited the live web pages.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the genuine external URLs, not local file paths (e.g., file://) or cached mockups.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GUI browser to capture the evidence rather than terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with a graphical web browser and its Developer Tools, rather than using terminal commands like curl, wget, or python scripts to download images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory is /home/ga/Documents/Forensic_Evidence. Required files are tor_wiki_infobox.png, tor_status_block.png, and capture_log.txt.
